hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The environment variable 'JDCI_CLI_URI_ALLOW_MALFORMED' must be set to the exact string 'yes'
The response body must exactly match '11' on the specific request sequence provided

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the same newline delimiters as in the app_isolation module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Separate consecutive output blocks with a single blank line character sequence

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_1_main_app_injection.json.description: Two independent applications are deployed simultaneously under different base paths (`/main` and `/secondary`). A request-scoped, container-managed bean carrying request-context injection points is injected into a resource in each application; the two applications' injection contexts must remain isolated. This covers the first (main) application: its resource exposes a path endpoint returning the matched relative path, and a header endpoint returning a custom request header value. Input requests `non-jaxrs-bean-injected/path/1` and `non-jaxrs-bean-injected/header/1` (with header `x-test`); output concatenates each request's status, routed URL, and body separated by a blank line.

feature10_2_secondary_app_injection.json.description: The second application deployed under base path `/secondary` proves the two coexisting applications keep isolated injection contexts. Its resource exposes a path endpoint returning the matched relative path, and a header endpoint returning a custom request header value, both under distinct sub-paths from the first application. Input requests `non-jaxrs-bean-injected/path/2` and `non-jaxrs-bean-injected/header/2` (with header `x-test`); output concatenates each request's status, routed URL, and body separated by a blank line.

feature11_response_filter_once.json.description: A response filter adds exactly one response header to every reply. Input requests the per-request echo endpoint and asks the harness to report how many times that header appears on the response; output reports status, routed URL, body, and `filter_invoked_count=1`, proving the filter runs exactly once per response.

feature1_constructor_param_binding.json.description: A resource is constructed with parameters bound from different parts of the HTTP request (path segment, query string, matrix parameter, request header) plus one value supplied by the container's value provider. The path segment names which bound value the resource should echo back as the response body. Input is a request specification with a path that ends in the name of the value to echo; output reports the response status, the fully routed request URL, and the echoed value.

feature2_reverse_echo.json.description: A qualified echo service reverses its input string. The service is selected by a qualifier among multiple implementations of the same echo interface. Input supplies the string to reverse via the `s` query parameter; output reports status, routed URL, and the reversed string as the response body.

feature3_stutter_echo_default.json.description: A qualified echo service repeats its input string a fixed number of times (default repeat factor is 2). Input supplies the string via the `s` query parameter; output reports status, routed URL, and the doubled string as the response body.

feature4_stutter_factor_set_get.json.description: The repeat factor of the stutter echo service can be reconfigured at runtime through its own HTTP endpoint, and the change is observed by the separate echo endpoint that shares the same application-scoped service instance. Input is a sequence of requests: a PUT that sets the factor (request body is the integer factor, response carries no content), followed by a GET on the echo endpoint. The output concatenates each request's status, routed URL, and body, separated by a blank line. A successful factor update returns status 204 with an empty body; the subsequent echo repeats the `s` query value `factor` times.

cases.expected_output:
status=204
url=http://localhost:9998/main/stutter-service-factor
body=

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/stutter?s=lincoln
body=lincolnlincolnlincoln

status=204
url=http://localhost:9998/main/stutter-service-factor
body=

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/stutter?s=lincoln
body=lincolnlincoln

feature5_1_per_request_echo.json.description: A request-scoped resource echoes the request context it was injected with: the full request URI, the `x` query parameter, and a per-instance counter that starts at 0. Because the resource is recreated for every request, the counter never advances beyond 0 across separate requests. Input supplies the `x` query parameter; the response body has the form `<request-uri>: queryParam=<x> 0`. Special characters in `x` appear decoded in the body but percent-encoded in the routed URL. Output reports status, routed URL, and that body.

feature5_2_dependent_per_request_echo.js.description: A request-scoped resource that is also a fully container-managed bean echoes the request context it was injected with: the full request URI, the `x` query parameter, and a per-instance counter that starts at 0 and never advances across separate requests. Input supplies the `x` query parameter; the response body has the form `<request-uri>: queryParam=<x> 0`. Output reports status, routed URL, and that body.

feature6_1_singleton_echo.json.description:
An application-scoped resource echoes its injected request context: the full request URI, the `p` path segment, and the `x` query parameter. Input supplies the `p` path segment and `x` query parameter; the response body has the form `: p=

, queryParam=`. Output reports status, routed URL, and that body.

feature6_2_singleton_counter.json.description: An application-scoped resource exposes a shared integer counter via a sub-path that can be read (GET, returns the current value then post-increments) and set (PUT, body is the new integer value, response carries no content). Because the resource lives for the whole application lifetime, the counter persists across requests. Input is a sequence of requests: set to 10, read (returns 10), read again (returns 11), set to 32, read (returns 32). Output concatenates each request's status, routed URL, and body separated by a blank line; a successful set returns status 204 with an empty body.

cases.expected_output:
status=204
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=10

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=11

status=204
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=32

feature6_3_singleton_exception.json.description: When the application-scoped resource's exception sub-path is requested, the resource raises a domain-specific exception that is translated by a registered exception mapper into a server-error response whose body names the exception category. Input requests the exception sub-path; output reports status 500, the routed URL, and the body `JDCIBeanException`.

feature7_1_dependent_singleton_echo.json.description:
An application-scoped resource that is also a fully container-managed bean echoes its injected request context: the full request URI, the `p` path segment, and the `x` query parameter. Input supplies the `p` path segment and `x` query parameter; the response body has the form `: p=

, queryParam=`. Output reports status, routed URL, and that body.

feature7_2_dependent_singleton_counter.j.description: An application-scoped managed-bean resource exposes a shared integer counter via a sub-path that can be read (GET, returns the current value then post-increments) and set (PUT, body is the new integer value, response carries no content). The counter persists across requests for the lifetime of the application. Input is a sequence of requests: set to 10, read (10), read (11), set to 32, read (32). Output concatenates each request's status, routed URL, and body separated by a blank line; a successful set returns status 204 with an empty body.

cases.expected_output:
status=204
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/dependent/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/dependent/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=10

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/dependent/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=11

status=204
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/dependent/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/jcdibean/dependent/singleton/alpha/counter?x=beta
body=32

feature7_3_dependent_singleton_exception.description: When the application-scoped managed-bean resource's exception sub-path is requested, the resource raises a distinct domain-specific exception that is translated by its own registered exception mapper into a server-error response whose body names the exception category. Input requests the exception sub-path; output reports status 500, the routed URL, and the body `JDCIBeanDependentException`.

feature8_extension_counter.json.description: A resource is injected with a shared container extension that exposes a monotonically increasing counter; each GET returns the next integer (1, 2, 3, ...). Input is a sequence of two GET requests; output concatenates each request's status, routed URL, and body separated by a blank line, with the second body strictly greater than the first.

cases.expected_output:
status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/counter
body=1

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/counter
body=2

feature9_producer_beans.json.description: A resource is injected with two beans supplied by producers: one produced from a producer field and one from a producer method. Each is exposed on its own sub-path and returns the value the producer assigned. Input requests sub-path `f` (field-produced, value `field`) and sub-path `m` (method-produced, value `method`); output concatenates each request's status, routed URL, and body separated by a blank line.

cases.expected_output:
status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/producer/f
body=field

status=200
url=http://localhost:9998/main/producer/m
body=method

_constraints.output_rule: The routed 'url' must be formatted as 'http://localhost:9998/{app_path}/{base_path}/{relative_path}']

_constraints.input_condition: Two applications ('main' and 'secondary') are deployed simultaneously
_constraints.output_rule: Resources and lifecycle contexts must remain completely isolated between 'main' and 'secondary' apps. Changes or state in 'main' must not leak to 'secondary'.
Output must be strictly formatted with status, url, and body, separated by newlines. Items concatenated across multiple requests have a blank line between them.

_constraints.input_condition: Response Filter is enabled and invoked
_constraints.output_rule: The final output block must include a line explicitly reporting the invocation count of the filter.

_constraints.input_condition: Handler raises a domain-specific exception (e.g., JDCIBeanException, JDCIBeanDependentException)
_constraints.output_rule: The response HTTP status must be 500, and the body must be the exact class name string of the exception.

_constraints.input_condition: Query parameter value contains a URL that is technically malformed for a URI (e.g., starts with 'http://' before existing URL)
_constraints.output_rule: The body must contain the malformed string 'http://' exactly as provided in the query param.
If the environment variable value is 'yes', requests to the malformed URI endpoint (C006) must return HTTP 200 instead of error.

_constraints.input_condition: Request is injected with a hand-written CDI instance (not managed by the service container)
_constraints.output_rule: The injected instance's `getInstance()` method must throw a RuntimeException with the message 'Object creation not allowed in JCDI'.
exact_values: Object creation not allowed in JCDI

_constraints.output_rule: If the setting is 'strict' and an app attempts to request more operations than supported (simulated overflow), logging must occur.
If the setting is 'yes', a broadcast mechanism must be attempted to announce configuration changes to connected clients.

_constraints.input_condition: Request hits a Singleton lifecycle bean
_constraints.output_rule: The bean must be created once per application, reused for all requests, and its internal state (like counters) must persist across requests.

_constraints.input_condition: Request hits a Request-Scoped lifecycle bean
_constraints.output_rule: A fresh instance must be constructed for every incoming HTTP request. State and counters must reset to initial values (e.g., 0) for each execution.

_constraints.input_condition: Path segment match 'pathParam' in constructor injection test
_constraints.output_rule: Response body must equal the raw path segment string ('pathParam').

_constraints.input_condition: Path segment match 'queryParam' and query contains 'q'
_constraints.output_rule: Response body must equal the query value (e.g., '123').

_constraints.input_condition: Path segment match 'matrixParam' and matrix contains 'm'
_constraints.output_rule: Response body must equal the matrix value (e.g., '456').

_constraints.input_condition: Path segment match 'headerParam' and headers contain 'Custom-Header'
_constraints.output_rule: Response body must equal the header value (e.g., '789').
Response body must equal the value produced by the ConstantProducer ('cdi-produced').

_constraints.input_condition: Request sent to 'reverse' path with qualifier 'reversing'
_constraints.output_rule: Response body must contain the query string 's' reversed. Special case: 'gogol' must return 'logog'.

_constraints.input_condition: Request sent to 'stutter' path with default qualifier and parameter 's'
_constraints.output_rule: Response body must be the string repeated twice by default. Overriding factor works if logic supports it.

_constraints.input_condition: PUT request to '.../stutter-service-factor' with body value '3'
_constraints.output_rule: Subsequent GET to 'stutter' must use the new repeat count (3 times, i.e., 'aaaaaaa').
GET request to counter path returns the current value and post-increments it.
PUT request to counter path sets the value and returns HTTP 204 with empty body.
Response body must be the string concatenated as: 'price=<value> : <normally_encoded_value>'. Price is calculated, normally_encoded is query param.

_constraints.input_condition: Path contains '/exception/' beneath a singleton/dependent bean
_constraints.output_rule: Must return error handling output formatted as `status=500, url=..., body={ClassName}`.

_constraints.input_condition: Edge case handling for budgeting/extension logic
_constraints.output_rule: No explicit test output specified for generic extension logic uniqueness, but extension instances must be unique.

Output format must strictly follow: `status=200
url=http...: body=http...: queryParam=<x> 0`
Counter values must increment persistently across requests (1 -> 10 -> 11 -> 32 -> 32).
No explicit test defined for verified runtime behavior of specific decorators, but infrastructure must support them.
Service must route to the correct internal handler.
Service must list from a list and return the identified constant.
Service must resolve interfaces from a map/list and return the identified ID/name. No explicit enum or value specified.
Service must resolve path and return the identified ID/name. No explicit enum or value specified.
Service must return the specific count associated with the endpoint.
Service must resolve the specific ID/name. Enum value not specified.
Service must expose the constants through the defined endpoint.

_constraints.input_condition: Access to a specific resource constant with key 'abc'
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the specific value associated with 'abc'.

_constraints.input_condition: Access to default service provider constants via `/providers/constants`
_constraints.output_rule: Service must expose the default provider constants.

_constraints.input_condition: Access to a default service provider with key 'abc'
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the specific value associated with 'abc'.

_constraints.input_condition: Access to a resource value via `/resources/{id}`
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the specific value associated with the requested ID.

_constraints.input_condition: Access to a service value via `/services/{id}`
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the specific value associated with the requested ID.
Service must return the specific value associated with 'service_abc'.

_constraints.input_condition: Invoking with 'all' key in service endpoint
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the value associated with 'all' (e.g., 'abc').
`ResourceConstants` must return 100 values, `ServiceProvider` must return 200 values, `ServiceConstants` must return 10 values. Format: 'ServiceInstance: <Name> ...'
Service must return a valid JSON string representing an exception or error state.
Service must return a text representation of the exception.

_constraints.input_condition: Execution requires the hard runtime assertions (#4, #5, etc.),
_constraints.output_rule: Service must function correctly despite these structural/runtime constraints.

_constraints.input_condition: Retrieving configured list of value names
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the list of strings provided in the test case configuration.

_constraints.input_condition: Retrieving configured map of key-value pairs
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the map of strings provided in the test case configuration.
Service must return the fully qualified class name string.

_constraints.input_condition: Requesting the result of a specific scenario ID
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the result of that specific test scenario.

_constraints.input_condition: Requesting the value associated with the key 'singleton-concept'
_constraints.output_rule: Service must return the constant exactly named 'singleton-concept'.
Service must return the enum value associated with 'singleton-inheritance' if defined.
Service must return the enum value associated with 'singleton-enumerator' if defined.
Service must return the enum value associated with 'singleton-binary' if defined.
Service must return the enum value associated with 'singleton-constants' if defined.
Service must return the enum value associated with 'singleton-extension' if defined.
Service must return the enum value associated with 'singleton-producers' if defined.
Service must return the enum value associated with 'singleton-interceptors' if defined.

hide.replacement: a specific CLI environment flag value — refer to the environment configuration
hide.oracle_response: The environment variable 'JDCI_CLI_URI_ALLOW_MALFORMED' must be set to the exact string 'yes'

hide.replacement: an increasing sequence of integer values — check the expected output log
hide.oracle_response: The response body must exactly match '11' on the specific request sequence provided
